name: Customer-support agent (chat/deflection)
description: LLM agent that resolves inbound support conversations end-to-end (answers; refunds; lookups) and escalates the rest.
key_challenges: confident wrong/hallucinated policy; prompt injection/jailbreak; pinning a comparable 'resolution' definition; safe escalation; over- vs under-deflection
eval_focus: resolution/deflection rate (pinned definition); groundedness; CSAT parity; escalation precision/recall; over-refusal; cost-per-resolution
example_companies: Klarna (OpenAI); Intercom Fin; Sierra; Air Canada (failure); Cursor 'Sam' (failure)
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Dominant failure is authoritative hallucination + jailbreak; so groundedness monitoring (AT-56); judge+calibration (AT-11/12); guardrails+refusal tuning (AT-65/66); incident-to-regression (AT-67); conversation transition-failure localization (EVL-15); outcome pricing (AT-74) all become load-bearing on top of the eval spine.

name: Voice agent (telephony)
description: Real-time speech agent (STT->LLM->TTS over telephony) handling calls: support; booking; intake; qualification.
key_challenges: sub-800ms end-to-end latency + turn-taking/barge-in; STT errors compounding; dual-control (guide a human through steps); mid-call task failure
eval_focus: end-to-end + per-hop latency (p50/95/99); barge-in time; containment rate; escalation accuracy; task-completion (state match); cost-per-call
example_companies: Retell; Vapi; Bland; PolyAI; Cresta; Sierra (tau2 Telecom)
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Adds a hard real-time constraint to UC-01: latency/SLO tasks (T-117; AT-59) and tool-call accuracy for in-call actions (AT-24/25) plus agent-loop budgets (AT-27/28) become primary, with the same safety+groundedness stack.

name: Coding agent / copilot
description: Generates; edits; and lands code from natural-language intent (autocomplete; IDE agents; autonomous issue->PR).
key_challenges: benchmark saturation + training-data contamination; scaffold variance (15-20 pts); compounding multi-file edits; perceived vs measured productivity
eval_focus: execution/test-pass on contamination-controlled private sets; acceptance/retention rate; PR-comment reduction; measured cycle-time; cost-per-task
example_companies: GitHub Copilot; Cursor; Devin (Cognition); SWE-bench/SWE-bench Pro; METR RCT
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Code is execution-checkable so deterministic graders (AT-10/EVL-07) and RL-env/sandbox/verifier tasks (AT-43/44/45) apply; contamination is the defining eval hazard (AT-47); fast frontier means constant capability probes/teardowns (AT-02/05/86); devtools PLG activation overlays attach (O-PLG-001/002/014).

name: Data-analyst / text-to-SQL / BI agent
description: Turns NL questions into governed warehouse queries and trustworthy answers over enterprise schemas.
key_challenges: syntactically-valid but semantically-wrong SQL; wrong join grain/row explosion; null/dedup errors; schema > context window; missing business logic
eval_focus: execution accuracy vs golden SQL (result-set match); schema-linking precision; groundedness (numbers trace to a query); %auto-answered vs routed
example_companies: OpenAI internal data agent; BIRD/Spider 2.0/BEAVER benchmarks; Contextual AI; Uber (internal)
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Golden-SQL execution grading is the defining eval (AT-10/EVL-07); schema retrieval/linking + retrieval-quality eval (AT-22/23); plan->validate->repair loop (AT-27); groundedness (AT-56); inherits the classic data-governance tasks (T-122/123/124/081/085).

name: RAG / enterprise search assistant
description: Question-answering grounded in a permissioned enterprise corpus; returns cited; permission-aware answers.
key_challenges: retrieval miss -> ungrounded answer; cross-source contradiction hidden behind a citation; permission leakage; stale/duplicate corpus; lost-in-the-middle
eval_focus: recall@k + groundedness measured separately from generation; citation correctness; faithfulness; permission-correctness (zero tolerance); freshness
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: RAG moves hallucination into retrieval; so retrieval-pipeline design + recall@k/groundedness eval (AT-22/23) and groundedness monitoring (AT-56) dominate; permission-aware retrieval forces entitlements + data-rights (T-048; AT-72); connector/MCP governance (AT-26); corpus contradiction is a data-quality job (T-085/121).

name: Document extraction & processing (IDP)
description: Extracts structured fields from invoices; contracts; forms; IDs; KYC packets with confidence-based human review.
key_challenges: silent field errors at scale; mis-calibrated confidence; missing line-items; layout/vendor drift; OCR on poor scans; weak feedback loop
eval_focus: field-level F1/extraction accuracy; straight-through-processing rate; confidence calibration; exception rate by doc type; correction throughput
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Output is structured/field-checkable so deterministic schema scorers (AT-10/EVL-07) and slice-by-doc-type evals (AT-14) are central; confidence-routing is a human-in-loop design (AT-60; T-054); bounding-box grounding (AT-56); lands in KYC/compliance overlays (O-FIN-001/006/011).

name: Sales copilot / AI SDR
description: Researches prospects; enriches data; personalizes; and runs outbound/CS motions (workbench; autonomous SDR; integrated platform).
key_challenges: generic 'obviously-AI' personalization; domain-reputation/deliverability damage at scale; stale enrichment; over-automation; compliance (CAN-SPAM/GDPR)
eval_focus: reply/positive-reply rate; meetings booked; bounce rate; inbox-placement/domain-health; personalization quality; pipeline/PQL conversion
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Quality bar is personalization judge (AT-11/12) but the binding constraint is a deliverability/'don't send junk' guardrail (AT-65/66) analogous to the consumer CTR-floor + opt-out discipline (O-CON-003/010); PQA/PQL scoring (O-B2B-006/O-PLG-005) and deal triage (O-B2B-003) drive targeting.

name: Enterprise content generation
description: Drafts brand-consistent marketing/business content at scale; on-brand and (in regulated settings) compliant.
key_challenges: off-brand voice; fabricated facts/claims in published content; generic 'AI slop'; wrong competitor claims; training-on-customer-data concerns
eval_focus: brand-voice adherence (judge+human); factuality/groundedness vs source-of-truth; edit/acceptance rate; compliance pass-rate; throughput
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Subjective brand-voice quality forces LLM-judge + calibration (AT-11/12) as the core eval; factuality/grounding (AT-56) + Knowledge-Graph RAG (AT-22) prevent fabricated claims; brand-safety guardrails (AT-65/66) and data-rights/consent (AT-72/85) for regulated content.

name: Meeting / conversation intelligence
description: Captures calls/meetings to produce transcripts; faithful summaries; action items; and (for sales) revenue-intelligence signals.
key_challenges: unfaithful summary (invented commitments); diarization/attribution errors; missing the key decision; recording-consent/privacy; training on call data
eval_focus: summary faithfulness + coverage (judge+human); action-item precision/recall; transcription WER + diarization; CRM-field accuracy; consent-compliance
example_companies: Granola; Gong; Fathom; Otter; Fireflies; Chorus/Clari
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Summarization eval is faithfulness/coverage (AT-11/12; AT-56 groundedness to transcript); recording introduces privacy + consent/disclosure (AT-72/85); maps to classic synthesis tasks (T-002 snapshot; T-145 verbatims; T-099/O-B2B-009 win-loss for the Gong job).

name: Agentic workflow automation
description: Multi-step; tool-using agent that executes a business process via plan-act-observe loops (IT/ops; back-office; 'do the whole task').
key_challenges: compounding/cascading error (0.95^20~36%); tool misuse; policy neglect; unbounded loops/cost; meltdown; irreversible wrong actions
eval_focus: pass^k/consistency (not just pass@1); end-to-end task completion (state match); per-transition success; tool-call accuracy; reliability decay; cost/steps
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Reliability (not accuracy) is the binding constraint; so harness/loop + budget design (AT-27/28); tool schema + tool-call accuracy + tool-reliability mining (AT-24/25/07); MCP governance (AT-26); transition-failure matrix (EVL-15); and safe-rollout/rollback for autonomous actions (AT-51/53; EVL-16) are all primary.

name: Recommendation system (generative/ML)
description: Predicts or (now) generates the next-best item for a user (feed; product; video; music); incl. generative recsys via Semantic IDs.
key_challenges: engagement-bait vs long-term retention; cold-start/long-tail; filter bubbles/feedback loops; online<->offline gap; serving cost/latency at scale
eval_focus: online engagement/retention A/B lifts (arbiter); offline recall/NDCG (proxy); segment effects; guardrails (diversity; retention); serving cost/latency
example_companies: YouTube (PLUM/Semantic IDs); Meta (HSTU); Kuaishou (OneRec); Netflix; Pinterest
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Abundant ground truth + online-experiment culture means classic experimentation (T-037/038/069/078/079/137) and cohort/retention analysis (T-013/014/112) dominate over LLM-judges; AI tasks are slice/cold-start evals (AT-14); bake-off (AT-29); online A/B of model change (AT-52); drift + serving economics (AT-57/59/70).

name: Search ranking & relevance
description: Orders results or supply to maximize a downstream outcome (web/site/e-commerce/marketplace search; the retrieval/ranking layer inside RAG).
key_challenges: optimizing clicks not outcomes; relevance vs coverage/long-tail supply; stale index; query-intent misread; ranking change that cuts liquidity without an A/B catching it
eval_focus: recall@k/NDCG (offline); search-to-fill or answer-success (online); time-to-result; per-query-type performance; A/B outcome lift with guardrails
example_companies: Glean (enterprise ranking); marketplaces (search-to-fill); TIGER/PLUM generative retrieval
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Bridges RAG retrieval (AT-22/23 recall@k) and online-experiment ranking (T-037/038/069/079; AT-52); slice by query type (AT-14); the marketplace matching overlays (O-MKT-003 match-rate; O-MKT-011 tune ranking to fill rate) are the consumer/two-sided analog.

name: Legal vertical copilot
description: AI for legal research; drafting; contract review/redlining; document Q&A; chronology over legal corpora.
key_challenges: citation hallucination (sanctionable); retrieving tangential law -> wrong rule; refusals/incomplete answers; overconfidence in a high-stakes domain; corpus coverage gaps
eval_focus: hallucination rate (and conditional-on-responding); accuracy vs expert baseline; citation correctness/groundedness; responsiveness vs refusal; task-specific accuracy
example_companies: Harvey; Thomson Reuters CoCounsel; LexisNexis Lexis+ AI; vLex Vincent; (Stanford RegLab; Vals VLAIR)
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Citation groundedness is the cardinal metric (AT-56; AT-22/23 legal retrieval); judge calibrated against lawyers (AT-11/12); adversarial bar-exam-style sets (EVL-11); red-team + pre-release accuracy/safety gate (AT-63/68); trust narrative + 'why is it wrong' (AT-82/83); firm buyers add security-questionnaire overlays (O-B2B-001/002).

name: Clinical documentation / health copilot
description: Ambient AI that listens to a visit and drafts the note for clinician review; plus clinical summarization.
key_challenges: hallucinated OR omitted clinical facts; side-conversation/noise capture; accent/specialty/acoustic degradation; over-homogenized notes; PHI handling; over-reliance
eval_focus: note accuracy incl. omissions vs clinician edits; edit/acceptance rate; time-saved + after-hours-EHR-time; burnout/well-being; specialty-sliced accuracy; PHI-safety
example_companies: Abridge; Nuance DAX Copilot; Ambience; Suki; Nabla; AWS HealthScribe
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Groundedness incl. omission detection is the safety-critical eval (AT-56); judge calibrated to clinicians (AT-11/12) + specialty slices (AT-14); clinician sign-off is human-in-loop by construction (T-054/055); clinician edits feed the flywheel (AT-60); PHI privacy + consent (AT-72/85; T-048); adoption read as burnout/acceptance (AT-84). No O-HLTH overlay exists yet (flagged).

name: Fraud & AML detection (fintech)
description: Scores transactions and onboarding for fraud and financial crime (AML) in real time; ML core + LLM-assisted investigation.
key_challenges: false negatives (regulatory/loss); false-positive overload; drift as tactics evolve; lagging fraud labels; unexplainable alerts failing audit; typology coverage
eval_focus: precision/recall (TPR vs FPR); alert volume vs review capacity; fraud-loss vs approval-rate trade-off; detection latency; drift; explainability/audit-readiness
example_companies: Feedzai (Jack Henry Defender); Hawk AI; FICO Falcon; NICE Actimize
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Most overlay-heavy use case: O-FIN-* is its native taxonomy (thresholds O-FIN-014; SAR O-FIN-004; fraud-vs-approval guardrail O-FIN-005; KYC O-FIN-001/011; audit trail O-FIN-006); precision/recall + drift (AT-14/57; T-033 guardrail; T-070/071 alerts); LLM-assist investigation maps to AT-08/11/56.

name: Trust & safety / guardrail layer (cross-cutting)
description: The policy/guardrail surface every other use case ships: define-what-it-must-never-do; filters; jailbreak + prompt-injection defense; PII; refusal calibration.
key_challenges: jailbreak/prompt injection; over-refusal (blocks valid use); under-refusal (allows harm); PII leakage; missing sanity checks on irreversible/financial actions; silent safety regressions
eval_focus: jailbreak/abuse-resistance vs versioned corpus; refusal vs over-refusal on paired benign/harmful sets; PII-leak rate; prompt-injection resistance; time-to-contain
example_companies: Chevrolet dealership (failure); Air Canada/Cursor (failure); Llama Guard/NeMo/Lakera class
why_it_stresses_these_tasks: Safety is a measurable; regressable; gated property: red-team + jailbreak corpus (AT-63/64); guardrail policy + refusal calibration (AT-65/66); incident triage + incident-to-regression (AT-67/17); pre-release safety gate (AT-68); adversarial sets (EVL-11); privacy/consent + regulated disclosure screens (AT-72/85; O-FIN-010); marketplace T&S gate (O-MKT-008).
